channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 67 replies — 146.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-05-17 07:29 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230578
>>62230495
if you want to talk serious share discord username

62230627
Based OP, a genuinely original effort thread with potential. Will continue to lurk for interest and hopes to obtain some knowledge, but this is out of scope

62232509
>>62230495
It had basis on ORB and VWAP but isn't just that.

>>62230578
Before any of that I'd like to hear your ideas on what you'd do / suggest for me to improve the strategy. I've already tried a lot of things, so I'd like to know how you could help me.

>>62230627
Thanks, but I haven't really gotten anything good out of this thread yet hahaha

62232797
bump

>>62230627
Also I thought about this a bit more, edge erosion is a real thing. The ironic thing is that if I released it with the intention of helping others, some actual firm might come along and pick that up and run with it. I recall there was this one strategy I was researching a while back. It performed well from 2016 all the way until 2020. But after 2020 till now it started performing worse and worse and worse. Until 2025 and 2026 where the returns were so ass that the only way you'd profit off of it was to essentially scalp it down to the ticks. Only a group with low latency and a huge bankroll would profit off of that. I saw live how a strategy had it's edge eroded.

I'm not claiming what I have is something that amazing. but that is a worry. At the most I'd share it with close friends and family and help them succeed. After all, what is the point of wealth if not to help others.

62233623
Bump

62234201
Bumpinonce more before I sleep

62236353
bump once more. is there one out here who's got the smarts?

62237934
>>62236353
all im going to say is your approach is too simple i thought orb had alpha decay years ago lol

62238015
>>62237934
Anon, I'd like some constructive feedback. Regardless of whether the approach is simple or not, the fact of the matter is that the data backs it up. Only one of the lines uses ORB.

The full blend, as shown in the first picture, is split across 5 different lines. The correlation between them, while not perfectly non-overlapping, is still low enough that it’s rare for them all to lose on the same day.

In the days since I made this, I was actually able to find a way to further cut down a few outliers, reducing total trades taken by 16% while increasing points by about 9%.

But these are just marginal improvements, and I’m hesitant to keep chasing them due to fears of overfitting.

My hypothesis is that, with how many trades I take, I should be able to quantitatively chop down the worst of them. I’ve already managed to do that with relatively simple filters over the past few days, but I’m running up against a wall, so I want to avoid going too far. THAT's why I made this thread. In hopes of finding someone with knowledge.

62238067
>>62238015
ok, now we're getting somewhere. you are afraid of overfitting and not even using ML lmfao noob

62238083
>>62238067
Rather than implement something I have no understanding of, I'm sticking to what I do know. I'll say it again. if you wanna offer criticism or advice, keep it constructive. just saying "ML" is so vague. Be more specific. ML in what way?

62238370
>>62238083
any sort of model.predict() is going to overfit unless you train on like 20-30% of the data thats implicitly going to be trained or predicted on, so with pure ta or price action strategies overfitting is never possible since theyre raw calculations of mathematics on price action time series data


holy shit anon you should know this im sharing liquid gold intuition with you

62238387
>>62219625
That equity curve is either a data issue or clearly just picking pennies up infront of a steamroller.
You being obssessed with "smoothness" is also a red flag.
You thinking "the data backs it up" with confidence is also a BIG red flag, this is not how real trading works.
Anyone can just run some lookahead biased regressions and uncover a "strategy", anything that is actually statistically robust and ACTIONABLE will be based off of forward walks and have effect sizes so small you'll be questioning whether its even real, and only THEN have you found something that might actually indeed be real.

62238401
>>62238370
I tried a variety of strategies based on price action but I could never get any of them to work. This setup is the best I could get. I tried other things but they wouldn’t work. I’m still not understanding how this ML would filter out losses in a way that’s actionable. If it just gives me some highly specific bucket like “trades between 0.75% and 0.84% of OR width are losing” then that’s just over fitting.

>>62238387
I’ve mentioned why. It’s for the sake of prop firms. They have hard drawdown rules. As mentioned before, i can theoretically pass and even get on a funded account with the strat as it is now. But it takes too long to pass and too long to get a payout. Most of what I read in ur response sounds like fluff no offense. I’m all for constructive criticism but I don’t see how any of what you said applies to my.

My strategy is a blend of 5 simple ideas that each trade on their own with the idea of:

Rather than find one single strategy that is good and trades often,
Find multiple good strategies that don’t trade often individually and then use them together.

I haven’t done any look back biased regressions, I have a separate 2026 only sample that I’ll test my strategies on to make sure they’re not overfit. And they’ll work just fine on new never before seen data.

Guys I am open to any constructive criticism, just please make it make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238500
>>62238401
Listen bro you don't just throw a couple of funny numbers in a computer and cash comes out thats just not how the world works.
You believe you need to be super opaque so we don't "steal" your strategy, but there are thousands of guys like you who think they have some top secret "strat" that they just need someone to fund them for.
What model are you even using? Some GARCH? HMM? Frequency of your data?

62238660
> top secret strategy trades the Nasdaq, an index that only goes up
> it makes money
> anon thinks he's a genius
> loses to buy-and-hold TQQQ

62238773
>>62238500
I’m not being opaque. I said I want advice from someone who has knowledge in fields related to statistics and quantitative analysis. Do you?

No, I don’t use GARCH. My strategy does not tune itself, it does not use some regressive or adjusting method. It follows the same rules trading in 2021 as it does in 2025 in the data set. The TP and SL’s are the only dynamic variable because they are based on VWAP, or ORB, or the volume profile as well as fixed % based on the open market value. A 20 point move in 2020 is far different than a 20 point move in 2025.
But they are not hand tuned or adjusted by me. It sets the TP based on the given range. It’s as simple as that.

I only used volume and OHLC of NQ and ES via 1m candles.

Also who the hell said I need funding? You should read all of my messages before making assumptions.

>>62238660
The worst drawdowns were approximately 3,000 points, while peak equity was around 50,000 points.

This is just napkin math, but let’s make a few simplifying assumptions:

Assume 1 NQ contract per trade. Some lines had multiple take-profits, but we’ll simplify. NQ is $20 per point, so a 3,000-point drawdown would require $60,000 in capital to withstand the worst observed drawdown. To be more conservative, let’s round that up to $100,000 of starting cash. Unexessary but let’s do it anyways.

Starting with $100,000, if running equity reaches 50,000 points, that’s 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000.

So, theoretically, starting with $100,000 and ending around $1 million is possible under these assumptions.

TQQQ went up about 230% from 2020 till 2025, where my data ends.

230% vs 900%.

If I had the money, I’d just put it all into SCHD and chill. But I don’t. That’s why I made this thread. That’s why I’m making this strategy and asking if anyone here can offer ideas that make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

62239204
>>62238500
lmfao, garch, mm, and HMM are all useless in trading

62239213
>>62238773
try this just for the lols and its like throwing shit to a wall hoping it sticks

code the strategy using an external data api use every talib indicator an oscilator then denoise the indicators then shove that as a filter and vectorize the backtest extract the highest sharpe ratio or net return in similar sharpe give or take in your datamining script

62239220
>>62239213
if you dont know anything about genetic algorithms watch coding trains NEAT flappy bird series its quite useful information and intuition for genetic algorithms doesnt have to be neuroevolution since these are just indicators you wont be topologically ranking these to another sorting neuron layer you just need to mutate and generate fitness effectively

62239225
if all of this is above your head just fucking quit quant isnt cut out for noobs its cut out for mathematics genius' and physicists

62239227
also i gave your ass free alpha i expect you to post source code now

62240221
>>62239213
I already tried something similar to this. I didn’t cover the entire library, but I used a more pointed approach that was computationally feasible for me and something I could actually verify myself.

The issue is that a lot of strategies perform all right, but they’re not smooth enough for a prop firm. What I’m showing is not the only thing I found, but it’s the smoothest thing I’ve found so far.

The others either weren’t as smooth or had large drawdown spikes.

Also there's no such thing as a single indicator that will perform well. If I do find sometrhing, it's going to be a combination of some, so I'd have to test a lot of permutations. Walking forward through millions of candles, testing hundreds if not thousands of combos, and then multiplying that again to find the optimal TP / SL setup. that is going to take a lot of computation and a hell of a lot of time of waiting. Not a smart approach.

Besides, I don't need a new strategy. I already have one. I just need to optimize it without suffering from overfitting. The only way I could do that is to have some new static filter. One thing I was looking at was introducing volumetrics and using that to filter bad trades, and it actually did help.

What I might do is try to combine the library with what I currently have and see if it improves in some way. Though that does open a whole can of worms. We'll see.


>>62239220
Flappy Bird is a clean fixed environment. trading ain't.

62240722
>>62240221
Nvm you can’t be helped you’re just trying to act smart at this point lmfao

62240724
bump once more before I wake up

62240823
>>62240221
do you even know why i recommended you to use a genetic algorithm? or you just blab shit like flappy bird if a fixed env? its about the intuition for GA's lol you are stupid as fuck nigga

62240835
>>62224277
Have you tried filtering on volatility anon? It tends to help trend following system. It could be something as simple as an inside bar chart pattern.

62240836
>>62240835
Don’t help him he’s retarded

62240887
>>62240835
I actually did try that. I tried filtering over a few fixed periods. However, all the trades that would be cut out would also include winners in an equal ratio. I couldn't really get any sort of volatility filter to cut out only losers.

I've been a bit hesitant to implement a filter that's based off of candlestick patterns, just because I think that's the weakest form of analysis. Analysis based on volumetrics or analysis based on trends or volatility, etc., that makes more sense to me compared to just analysis based on “price is forming an inverted cup or an upside-down H”

That's been my results from a lot of filters. All the filters will cut out trades, but they'll cut out an equal amount of wins and losses. Or if they do cut out more losses, the ratio is not really that much. I am still sure that there is some method that I could use, because I'm averaging eight trades a day. In my mind, surely there has to be a way to cut that down to just two and drastically increase win rate.

item.title: I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-17 07:54 UTC

OP

I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. What the fuck do i do now? I don't even like what I do

Early replies (first 3)

62236318
>enter thread
>download op image
>leave thread

62236322
>>62236309
Sex!

62236326
>>62236309
I deserve to have sex with women who look like this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236330
>>62236309
At least you’re not a 30 year old entry level analyst dealing with retarded ass sales reps all day long. I bet you get paid better too.

62236409
>>62236309
That is a job for someone literally a decade younger than you. Why not just get an AE job?

62236420
>>62236409
I might have at this point. My current employer is Gate keeping me from getting an AE promotion. I've applied so many times for remote AE positions , but they won't hire you unless you have previous AE experience. I might have to start applying to on site roles now which will suck, but at least if they accept me then I'll have some experience

62236517
>>62236309
I'm 33 and work in Walmart as a stocker

62236592
>>62236420
It's cuz you're (literally) competing against 500-1000+ applicants per role, even including the on-site roles. And no, baseddit strategies like
> just go to the last page on linkedin bro, less applicants to compete against that way bro
> you can't give up bro! it's a numbers game bro!
will not work because everyone else out there is already using the same strategy, hence the flood of applicants per job which make it impossible to even get an interview. And for that linkedin point, used to work but not anymore because every job posted on there has the stupid "promoted" tag which exposes it to the entire globe of applicants within seconds. Each job posting gets bot applicant flooded within micro seconds. Many of these applicants also have rockstar-tier resumes too so good luck competing against them.

tldr; job market is PERMANENTLY fucked, thank AI and globalism for this mess

62236645
>>62236309
>33
>entry level
nigga you lost a whole decade of your life you need to figure out what happened there

62236684
>>62236318
kek

62236700
>>62236517
How long have you been working there? Was it easy to get the job?

62236733
>>62236517
Become a stonker.

62237056
>>62236309
just get promoted to AE like the rest of the sales monkeys. its not any harder. literally apply yourself to applying for better roles the way you prospect for opportunities in your day job u sped

62237118
>>62236318
literally me

62237179
>>62237118
It's some kind of long format ai clip I found but didn't save in time
Shit is hot af

62237198
>>62237179
there you go

62237427
>>62237198
This AI shit is to gooners what crack cocaine was to black neighbourhoods in the 80s. We are not far off from everyone just being able to generate their 10/10 goon material at the press of a button and edge 24/7. It's Joever (at least for your prostates)

62237463
>>62237198
>>62237427
Coomer here. Maybe I'm just old, but AI shit kills my boner the moment I see it. I can't get over the uncanny valley feeling.

62237611
>>62237198
Do you have that one where a goth egirl poses with a boomer, then the scene switches to them both covered in sweat like they both had rough sex?

62237614
>>62237427
24/7 gooning sounds bullish for cloud providers and electricity generation.

62239211
>>62237463
Same it's just not there yet.

62240812
Even at forty I still dream about getting a job with prospects where I can grow. i work just above minimum wage. What choice do I have ? Just admit everything is lost ?

62240913
>>62237198
can you make her to sit on a girthy bbc?

62240917
>>62236309
You Can Do Robinhood With Me! I Am Graham Stephaaaaaannn!!!!!!!!!! I Also Buy A Couple Of Sandwiches Every Week!

item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-17 07:38 UTC

OP

>allows schizophrenia to exist for some reason
>doesn’t make LINK go up for some reason

Early replies (first 3)

62236464
niggas be worshipping the demiurge and calling him hashem in dey prayers sheiiit

62236489
>>62236130
>Tests you by pumping LINK from $0.17 to $52
>You commit the sin of greed and don't sell
>Now you're in bagholder hell

Deserved

62236515
>>62236489
Real, it was a test and I failed, now my real life can never begin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236558
i know right.
god is so cringe

62236575
>>62236464
broads be like yeah God isn't real but by the way i confessed my sins to my therapist and she forgave me because my ESTP type B personality really clashed with the bad energy set off by the Capricorn alignment so she's upping my ADHD autism bipolar meds

62239270
>>62236130
get rid of your schizio as soon as possible it isnt high iq i went through that shit without selling my soul for it or having predisposition someone else a witch made me get it and it was fucking horrible 5.5 years of my life i lost 2m of crypto and shit i couldve been worth 1b right now

62239272
>>62239270
neither is depression, anxiety, or ADD, adhd is extremely high iq i have it my g is 130

62240711
>>62236130
Linkies, like many bag holders, aren't schizophrenic at all. You're autistic, incapable of updating your worldview no matter how much evidence to the contrary there is. Larping as schizophrenic is your way of doubling down on your inflexibility.

62240872
>>62236130
schizophrenic here ask me anything

62240880
>>62240872
will link go up?

62240898
>>62240711
Nah some Linkies are definitely schizo. They literally believe Bulgarians and Bitcoin whales are suppressing their rank 16 premined shitcoin kek

pls rember that wen u feel scare or frigten
never forget ttimes wen u feeled happy

wen day is dark alway rember happy day

62240829

AI, and especially ChatGPT, will call you the smartest most misunderstood person on the planet even if you have an IQ of 85.

It’s a major problem with schizophrenics using it, because the AI makes them think that their delusions are actually real

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 94 replies — 38.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-05-17 05:29 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709

Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109

do the alternative. Privacy

62234248

Government is shit.

62234899

I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090

give me money

62237423

you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

62238954
>>62234248
I'm not surprised lol

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

62240786
>>62228616

item.title: PAIN — 27 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
PAIN

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-17 05:59 UTC

OP

How do I cope with the fact that if I had just invested $5k in SANDISK a year ago, that I would have had over $100k and solved all of my money problems and been blissfully happy in life?
I want off of this fucking ride...Fuck my luck. There may never ever be another opportunity like this ever again, and I'll just remain poor for the rest of my life as inflation gets worse and worse.

Early replies (first 3)

62236172
95k isn't going to solve your problems. the idea that you think it will is your problem.

62236202
>>62236172
>t.richfag
Richfags are always so delusional...It would solve all my problems when it comes to money, so yes it would.

62236227
>>62236202
If you're retarded enough that you think 95k is life changing money you're retarded enough to get into infinitely more money problems once you solve your current ones. Case in point: you really think you would have let that 5k sit in sandisk up until now, not realizing you would have taken so much out it would be more like 30k with 20k in realized profits, at best. Then you'd hold it until it became 15k bringing your grand total to 35k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236252
>>62236227
Actually more like you'd have like 7k with 20k of realized profits because you're so desperate for money you'd be harvesting half every time it doubled, at least

62236261
>>62236202
No not, at all. You'd just gamble and lose on the next thing. Poorfag is a stinky cologne that everyone smells.

62236265
>>62236147
There's probably another SANDISK out there right now you can invest 5k in and 20x in 2 years. Good luck finding it though!

62236310
>>62236227
It is life changing money, because I wouldn't be a wagecuck like you, working for decades just to get even close to that amount.
>>62236252
>because you're so desperate for money you'd be harvesting half every time it doubled
Wrong. I would have let it sit.
>>62236261
>You'd just gamble
But enough about you wagecuck. Wagecucks always smell the worst.
>>62236265
Thank you for giving me HOPE anon :) I'll keep my head up.

62236343
>>62236147
There is another sandisk somewhere right now

62236411
>>62236310
says the guy crying in the casino

62236419
>100k
>solve your problems
Oh sweet summer child, in this economy?

Maybe 750k could solve your problems, Not a measly 100k doe.

62236636
>>62236147
NVAX go to 300$ in 1/3 years.32X
PATH go to 160$ in two years. 16X

62238283
>>62236636
NVAX
What the fuck is that chart? lmao

62238995
>>62236147
>There may never ever be another opportunity like this ever again
Me every time I look back at investments I Could have made, except I couldnt because I didnt have any money either

man I could have put in all my liquid cash I have on hand a year ago and put it all in sandisk too, what did they even do to make a 3380% increase last year ? except hte meme shortage

62239005
>>62236147
>in november december we KNEW and saw clear signs of memory shortage
>stock was still low
how hard is investment really? we all have plenty of time and clear signs to buy in at like 200 usd
it's clear signs and easy as hell to buy early you had so much time really
I was busy doing other shit though
it seems so easy every time especially when you have clear signs like this I was watching memory prices and looking at componenets too

62239865
>>62236147
I was this close to starting to invest a year ago and just pumping all my money in but then again I don't have enough money security to do so yet so it seemed like a bad thing
>miss sandisk
>miss intel
>miss everything
I would have been better off

62240540
>>62236147
you buy bitcoin now and know that whatever you put into it will 50x

62240544
>>62236202
get a job and earn 90k then lol

62240574
>>62236265
>>62236343
>>62236147

it's probably SIVE. just saying. Yes, you're late, but only late-early, not late-late.

62240608
>>62236147
I don't understand why schizophrenics mourn in retrospect decisions they never could have recognized at the time.

are you stupid? how have you not already died over all the lottery drawings you would have won if you had just known the numbers like you do now?

62240618
>>62236419
that's the second time you've written 'sweet summer child' with that fucking gif
can't tell if you're trolling or you're actually a redditor

62240622
>>62236265
This OP. Buy the upcoming SpaceX IPO

62240629
>>62236147
yeah and i could be a millionaire now. in probably a multi-millionaire. anyone who looks at a stock price could be. i coulda bought netflix when it was two bucks lol.
thats just how it goes. no one has a crystal ball. only insiders and congress KNOW when a certain stock or industry is going to pump or dump. try not to let your lack of precognition trouble you too much.

62240630
>>62236147
because you had no way to know sandisk was going to pump. it could have just as likely gone bankrupt or gone up by 5%
hindsight is 20/20. don't believe me? what stock is about to 20x now? see you have no fucking clue, nobody does except a small number of jewish insiders.

62240682
>>62236147
Put 5k into atm puts monday and u will make 100k

62240694
>>62236147
>>62236202
>he thinks low 6 figure hell will fix his life
Bro... at least aim for low 7 figure purgatory

62240753
>>62236147

62240821
>>62236147

62238332

62238351
>>62238330

62238706
>>62238330
so many Chainlink partnerships!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238767
>>62238330
just waiting when it drops to $5

Linkie blueniggers will be on suicide watch

62238771
cuck world will be destroyed
no more cuck world

62239124
>>62238767
I have 300k of dry powder I will buy with if that happens. I m extremely anti-semetic, do you Jews want this? Do you want me to but 60k chainlink tokens?

62239132
>>62239124
oh no.....I bet the Jews are trembling in fear knowing that you're a baggie with 60k useless tokens

62239136
>>62238330

62239140
>>62239136
Link and BNB were trading at the same price in 2017.

kek baggie

62239175
>>62238330
>DTCC: we're using CCIP
>-40.04%
This token must be cursed or manipulated
>inb4 token not needed
Cope. Any other project with useless chucky cheese tokens would have a throbbing pump in light of such news.

62239282
>>62239140
soul darkening post

62239682
fuddies were unironically sweating bullets in the 10 dollar range what a bunch of retarded pussies lol

62239700
>>62239140
if i would have held my bnb i would have 40 million m, but i swappwd it all for link in defi summer

62239725
>>62239682
Once again the entire world conspired to get Link back under $10 amid a barrage of insane news.
First Bitcoin dumped but Link rebounded back above $10 pretty quickly.
And then of course the stock market dumped the next day.

I just don't understand how this keeps happening.

62239748
>>62239725
Disengenous nigger. Bitcoin had already been dumping for 2 hours prior to that announcement.

62239771
>>62239748
Because Link was above $10 and showing strength after the DTCC announcement the day before.
And the second that Fidelity news dropped, BTC just fell off a cliff. Are you blind or something.

62239816
>THE cuckolds of crypto
>Actually one of the coins that are doing best compared to their 2023 bottom

62239845
>>62239771
I thought link wasn't allowed over $9 tho. That was the rallying cry of the linkies for months on end during 2025 (Jamie pull that up). Now suddenly the $10 level is the latest price level that the jews don't want you to know about.
What if... Chainlink is just going up and down with the rest of the altcoins?

62239855
>>62239140
>if LINK doesn't outperform every single asset on earth then it's bad

62239857
Is there even a fucking backstory to this shit? I've never seen this shit coin shilled IRL, on CNBC, bloomberg, ANYWHERE. It's literally just /biz/. So are people actually bag holding this shit, or is just a couple of jeets who spam this board day in and day out for the fuck of it?

62239872
>>62239855
it's just bad

62239880
>>62238330
Price + chart

62239887
>>62239140
MOGGED

62239890
>>62239872
> cherrypicks a timeframe (5Y) where 99% of altcoins are down
>LINK is down
impressive, very nice

62239892
>>62239890
Holy shit new cuckold cope just dropped. 5years is now a “cherry picked” time frame. We should all zoom out to 2017 if we have to analyse Link’s price performance lol

62239908
>>62239892
kek but if you do the same for xrp they FREAK out

62239909
>>62239892
>We should all zoom out to 2017 if we have to analyse Link’s price performance lol
do it bitch

62239920
>>62239816
Fuddies will only talk about the 5Y timeframe because all coins are down in that timeframe.
Or maybe they all bought the top.

62240032
>>62239872

62240069
>>62239845
>I thought link wasn't allowed over $9 tho. That was the rallying cry of the linkies for months on end during 2025 (Jamie pull that up).
It was $9 too yes.
It's any fucking resistance

>>62239857
>I've never seen this shit coin shilled IRL, on CNBC, bloomberg, ANYWHERE.
Check Swift's or DTCC's socials and websites, for starters.

62240159
>>62240069
Didn't the CTO of SWIFT have to step down because all of their posts being swarmed by retards with apu avatars asking about the price action of LINK?

62240360
>>62239725
Its because Chainlink will be a major part of the new financial order. Its absolutely FUCKING imperative that people are not looking into these type of things. You have NO FUCKING IDEA what is coming. I’m honestly scared as fuck for the new world order.

62240731
>>62239920
>>62239909
Why are cuckolds like this?

Your shitcoin got outperformed by Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB, Dogecoin, Solana, Monero, Ripple and even Stellar Lumens.

Take the L dumb baggies

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62240440

62240447
>>62240429

If they do that I'll stop fighting against the orange menace

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62240472
that's funny because my sources say that they're gonna plummet

62240474
>>62240472
that’s even funnier my sources say their going to plummet the stock to fake people out and then buy

62240478
>>62240429
bears btfo again

62240483
>>62240474
Yeah but my sources say that it'll be a bull trap, and the real plummet will begin

62240500
>>62240478
Majestic painting

62240520
>>62240429
If even the Kobussy letter is posting bullish news it's bad for bears. This is the inverse equivalent of Axios reporting that the west has fallen.

62240541
>>62240429
OOOO

62240558
>>62240429
buy an ad.

62240599
>>62240483
I've got sources that are quite sure line goes up.

62240605
Translation: stocks about to dump hard Monday

62240637
>>62240447
sorry chud, amazon paid good money to have those exemptions removed and they're not about to let their goycattle servant trump undo that.

62240695
>>62240429
> lower tariffs means less income
> higher deficit
> lower bond prices
> higher yields
> more expensive debt
> lower equity

So no.

62240717
>>62240429
if it was true, btc would be blood green right now

62240730
>>62240429
Hmmm nyonyonyom unspecified products! I am a good consooomer, just aped into lifesavings saaars!

62240761
>>62240429
>China and the United States have agreed to expand agricultural trade through tariff reductions and tackle non-tariff barriers and market access issues, China's commerce ministry said on Saturday after this week's summit in Beijing.

>China's farm imports from the U.S. still face an additional 10% levy after last year's rounds of tit-for-tat tariffs sharply curtailed trade, which fell 65.7% year-on-year to $8.4 billion in 2025, according to U.S. Department of Agriculture data.

>Market watchers expect a 10% cut in basedbean tariffs, which could allow private Chinese crushers to resume purchases that were largely sidelined during last year's U.S. harvest, when state crop traders were the only buyers.

>"Tariff reductions on agricultural products would mark a normalization of China-U.S. farm trade, allowing commercial buyers to re-enter the market," said Johnny Xiang, founder of Beijing-based AgRadar Consulting.

>The ministry said both sides agreed to "resolve or make substantive progress" on non-tariff barriers and market access issues.

>Beijing on Friday granted five-year registration extensions to 425 U.S. beef plants that had largely been shut out after their registrations lapsed last year, and approved new five-year registrations for 77 additional U.S. facilities.

>U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Friday the U.S. expects China to buy "double-digit billions" worth of U.S. farm goods over the next three years, although neither side has yet released details on specific products, values or volume.

Agricultural stocks

62233643
>>62233522

62235083

62239779

62239800

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62239801
fade it

62239838
>>62239800
Oil is one of the unspecified product. The Gulf of America is open for business.

62239932
>>62239759
I don't care about the S&P500. I want MU to resume its march upwards into the sunny climes of fuck you money.

62239946
>>62239759
This makes me want to sell.

62239947
>>62239759
Its called The Crack Up Boom

62239997
>>62239759
why won't it go up? why is btc down?

62240031
>>62239838
Based
I remember voting for Trump on his "Drill American oil and Sell It to China" policy. His Make China Great Again platform is exactly why everyone flocked to him.

62240052
>>62239759

62240075
>>62239759
DRAM needs to print this week. Spy calls monday

62240743
>>62239759
Esiest 100x eoy saars!

62240754
>>62239759
>China and the United States have agreed to expand agricultural trade through tariff reductions and tackle non-tariff barriers and market access issues, China's commerce ministry said on Saturday after this week's summit in Beijing.

>China's farm imports from the U.S. still face an additional 10% levy after last year's rounds of tit-for-tat tariffs sharply curtailed trade, which fell 65.7% year-on-year to $8.4 billion in 2025, according to U.S. Department of Agriculture data.

>Market watchers expect a 10% cut in basedbean tariffs, which could allow private Chinese crushers to resume purchases that were largely sidelined during last year's U.S. harvest, when state crop traders were the only buyers.

>"Tariff reductions on agricultural products would mark a normalization of China-U.S. farm trade, allowing commercial buyers to re-enter the market," said Johnny Xiang, founder of Beijing-based AgRadar Consulting.

>The ministry said both sides agreed to "resolve or make substantive progress" on non-tariff barriers and market access issues.

>Beijing on Friday granted five-year registration extensions to 425 U.S. beef plants that had largely been shut out after their registrations lapsed last year, and approved new five-year registrations for 77 additional U.S. facilities.

>U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Friday the U.S. expects China to buy "double-digit billions" worth of U.S. farm goods over the next three years, although neither side has yet released details on specific products, values or volume.

It's purely about agricultural products, this is aimed at helping the biggest welfare recipients in the US, farmers, they're also largely Trump supporters, so Trump is having to wind back his tariffs as it's hurting farmers and consumers as a whole, maybe these retarded farmers shouldn't rely on a crop destined for a single market that's the main rival of the US

62240760
>>62240754
dependent on a single market*

62238604

62238614
You are in adult daycare, follow their rules

62238615
>>62238604
we have a government mandated profile picture for our OWN PERSONAL SOCIAL MEDIA ACCOUNT
and my fucking pension is on the stake if i dont bend the knee

62238618

talk to your union rep, they're violating your 1st

not that it matters, remember vaccine mandates

what you do on your own time and computer is your own business. As you were. Carry on.

62238626
>>62238618
you know that shit wont work and so am i.
im just here to vent and asking for you /biz/tard thoughts and prayer that these boomer would die sooner.

62238636
>>62238626

anyways, I guess this isn't US government
they do some stupid shit but this sounds a bit extra even for them

62238662

62238678
>>62238615

62238685

62238693
>>62238615
Post the image but what the hell my man lmao

62238708
>>62238599

62238867
>>62238662

>>62238693
>>62238685
I tried redacted everything
>>62238708

62239170
We can't all make it. Now shut up and make my life easier

62239190
>>62238867
holy FUCK I wouldn't even give them my notice I would straight up stop showing up KEKK
what the FUCKK

62239226
>>62238867
FUCKING KEK HAHAHAHA

62239959
>>62238867

62239964
>>62238867

thank you for this

62240243
>>62238867

62240293
>>62238867

62240343
>>62238599

62240366

item.title: Why do you exist BOBO? — 21 replies — 3.4/hr

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-17 01:46 UTC

OP

It always goes up! ALWAYS!
We Mumus chuckle when you make posts on this board. Like a deranged Hobo taking about the end times and later on sucking cock in an alley for a sandwich.

62239977
All of those events pale in comparison to what's coming and there's no guarantee it continues to go up.

62239981
>>62239976
Selection bias.
Muh US stock market.
Yeah like this century is gonna be like the last one.
Check out how other stock markets (not US) have done, nigger.
It's NOT always "up".

62239983
>>62239976
But we are near the end of american empire. So yeah, this time is different.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62239985
>>62239977

vietnam was bigger than anything that happened after

62239991

62240035
>>62239976

62240042
>>62239976

62240076
>>62240035
>>62240042

62240093
>>62239976
bears are failed investors. never forget that.

62240098
>>62240076
the part no one mentions is there is a big difference between investing 10k a year in an etf and 100k a year. low earners dont make it to the top 5% or anywhere close.

62240101
All I got to say is if you don't have at least 40-50% of your portfolio after seeing what the future of robotics and AI is showcasing right now in 2026, you gotta be retarded.

62240163
>>62239976

62240213

62240217

62240239

62240261

62240341
>>62239977
oldfag insider from the 1500s here this anon is right, imagine buying anything after the 19th century lmao couldn't be me

enjoy getting dumped on.by the founding forefathers

62240372
>>62239976
Smart BOBOs are fine since they take advantage of dips to buy more. Bad BOBOs just try to demoralize others.

62240387
>>62239983
You are so right anon. The American elites that own the world will let the empire that enables their evil to collapse, and hand the world to China, probably. AHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62240406
>>62240387
All global super powers have fell the same way the past 2000 years.

62240420
>>62240042
The difference is the US prints money like crazy under the disguise of their (((inflation))) metrics
The total market goes up as every year consumers get squeezed more to eek out higher profit margins. If the system collapses then everything becomes worthless including your stash of gold.

item.title: Kraken has moved to CCIP. — 59 replies — 2.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kraken has moved to CCIP.

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-16 11:22 UTC

OP

kek fuddies

Early replies (first 3)

62230910
>>62230891
Time to dump btc from 81 and eth from 2300

62230914
WAGMI WAGMI

62230921
>shovel salesman announces that shovels are useful, actually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282
This so much, all the top financial institutions of the world are getting absolutely gobblecocked by sergey

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

62236073
>>62231115
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62236163
>>62231853
This is old news isn't it? Lido was already with CCIP. They're just now publishing their rationale behind the decision.

62236196
>>62236073
doing great thanks
oh btw: pee plus see, bitch

62236449
>>62231646
>i have already won at life

yeah for a guy that's "winning" you don't seem very happy about that

>>62231646
>hope you fixed your porn/masturbation addiction though

you're addicted to keeping track of a random online personality jacking off
dude that's fucking wild

have you told anyone irl?

62236468
>>62236449
Hope you're doing well, Adem

62236472
hey guys --- how's the new "integration" going today? lets check the numbers shall we?


>CCIP fees last 24h: $637

bahahahaha

>Link price action last 24h: -5.2%

bahahahahahahahaah holy shit


nobody, and i mean NOBODY, gets viciously c u c k h o l d e d quite like the boyscouts.... and then comes back time and time again, absolutely BEGGING for more

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62236479
>>62230910
Oh my fuckk you weren't even lying.

I hate this gay ass market.

62236498
>>62231117
You know he probably owns more Link than almost anyone else here, hes just be a dickhole for fun because he knows whats coming.

62236508
>>62230891
i saw kraken now allows margin gambling without having to have a huge portfolio. pretty cool imo. as far as link, i don't think about it at all

62236594
>>62236163
Well here's a completely new one.

62236605
OMFG WE ARE MOONING!!!!!

62236616
HOLY FUCKING SHIT IT JUST KEEPS GOING!!!!!

62236626
